# Loomle

Loomle is an agent-native Unreal Engine integration. It turns complex editor
state into SAL, the Structured Agent Language: compact ordered text that people
and agents can read, copy, query, and patch without replacing UE's native
object model.

Canonical user documentation:

- Home: https://loomle.ai/
- Install: https://loomle.ai/install.html
- Quickstart: https://loomle.ai/quickstart.html
- Core concepts: https://loomle.ai/concepts/
- MCP calls: https://loomle.ai/calls/
- Interfaces: https://loomle.ai/tools/
- Workflows: https://loomle.ai/workflows/
- Source: https://github.com/loomle/loomle

Availability and installation:

- Loomle 0.7 will ship only through Fab, but its public package is not yet
  available. The current Fab listing still distributes 0.6:
  https://www.fab.com/listings/f0fb545c-b1d9-4525-8642-3f170134c428
- The first accepted 0.7 QA target is UE 5.7 on Apple Silicon macOS.
- The plugin contains the Bridge and self-contained Client at
  `Resources/Loomle/<platform-arch>/loomle(.exe)`.
- No website installer, Python, uv, Node.js, global Loomle install, separate
  project Client, or session-attachment daemon is required.
- When upgrading from 0.6, close Unreal Editor and remove or move the old
  `<Project>/Plugins/LoomleBridge` out of every affected project's `Plugins`
  directory. A project plugin shadows the Fab engine plugin.

Five public MCP tools:

- `project`: inspect projects and bind one MCP session to one project.
- `sal_query({ text })`: execute one self-contained SAL Query Text.
- `sal_patch({ text })`: dry-run or apply one ordered SAL Patch Text.
- `sal_schema({})`: return the active interface-module index.
- `sal_schema({ module })`: return one static interface card.
- `editor_context({})`: return the current UE interaction target as ordered SAL
  Object Text.

Six active SAL interfaces:

- Asset
- Blueprint
- Class
- Graph
- StateTree
- Widget

Recommended workflow:

1. Bind one project.
2. Locate the exact target.
3. Query a compact view.
4. Discover static or dynamic schema only when needed.
5. Use a UE Palette capability before creation.
6. Dry-run the complete Patch.
7. Apply the same authored Patch and read back.
8. Compile and save through the owning asset.

Do not guess UE Classes, fields, Pins, object ids, destinations, Palette
entries, or operation names. The Client's `sal_schema` interface cards and
exact `with schema` results are authoritative for the installed build.
